name: nCino, Inc.
business: nCino provides a cloud-based intelligent banking platform that helps financial institutions optimize operations by embedding banking intelligence into the tools FI employees already use. The platform offers onboarding, account opening, lending, credit monitoring, and AI-powered analytics solutions. nCino sells to financial institutions of all sizes globally.

exposures.sentence: nCino helps FIs of all sizes optimize their operations by embedding banking intelligence directly into the tools FI employees already use.
AI is reshaping the financial services industry, and nCino is helping FIs navigate the change effectively.

We depend on data centers operated by or on behalf of Salesforce, AWS, and other third parties, and any disruption in the operation of these facilities could adversely affect our business and subject us to liability.
Our business faces significant risks from diverse and evolving cybersecurity threats, including increased threats from the use of AI/ML.

catalysts.event: CEO transition: Pierre Naud retired as Chairman and CEO, Sean Desmond appointed President and CEO
catalysts.sentence: Effective February 1, 2025, Pierre Naud retired as the Company's Chairman and Chief Executive Officer and Sean Desmond was appointed to succeed Mr. Naud as the Company's new President and Chief Executive Officer and as a member of the Company's Board of Directors.

On the Sandbox Acquisition Date, we acquired Alphapack, Co. dba Sandbox Banking (Sandbox Banking), a digital transformation leader serving the financial services industry, for an aggregate purchase price of $62.9 million, inclusive of an additional earn-out opportunity of up to $10.0 million.

On May 27, 2025, we announced a workforce reduction of approximately 7% and office space reductions in certain markets (collectively, the 2026 Restructuring Plan) in furtherance of our efforts to improve operational efficiencies.

In March 2025, our Board of Directors authorized a stock repurchase program of up to $100.0 million of our outstanding common stock (the March 2025 Stock Repurchase Program) which we completed in the third quarter of fiscal 2026.

In December 2025, our Board of Directors authorized the December 2025 Stock Repurchase Program of up to $100.0 million of our outstanding common stock pursuant to which we repurchased 1.0 million shares of our outstanding common stock for $25.0 million, excluding transaction costs and excise tax associated with the repurchases, in fiscal 2026.

catalysts.event: Incremental Facility Amendment for $200 million term loan
catalysts.sentence: Subsequent to January 31, 2026, we entered into an Incremental Facility Amendment to the 2024 Credit Agreement for a senior secured incremental term loan of $200.0 million.

catalysts.event: Additional $100 million accelerated share repurchase program effective March 31, 2026
catalysts.sentence: Subsequent to January 31, 2026, we entered into an additional $100.0 million share repurchase program to be completed under an accelerated share repurchase program effective March 31, 2026.
